title: pinus strobiformis subsp. veitchii
intro: Pinus strobiformis subsp. veitchii, also known as the Veitch's Colorado spruce, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the high-altitude regions of the Rocky Mountains in North America. The essential oil extracted from its leaves has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Pinus strobiformis subsp. veitchii include its ability to reduce stress, improve respiratory issues, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other topical applications.
